briefdescription.para: Writes an ERROR level message to the log.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to report severe errors, such as those from which it's not possible to recover.

parameterdescription.para: A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Writes a WARNING level message to the log.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to register messages related to unusual situations that are not necessarily errors.

parameterdescription.para: A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Writes an INFO level message to the log.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to write generic user oriented messages.

parameterdescription.para: A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Writes a DEBUG level message to the log.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to write developer oriented information.

parameterdescription.para: A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

memberdef.name: LOG_WRN_ONCE

briefdescription.para: Writes a WARNING level message to the log on the first execution only.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant for situations that warrant investigation but could clutter the logs if output on every execution.

parameterdescription.para: A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Writes a WARNING level message to the log with rate limiting.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant for situations that warrant investigation but could clutter the logs if output too frequently. The message will be logged at most once per default interval (see CONFIG_LOG_RATELIMIT_INTERVAL_MS).

parameterdescription.para: A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Writes an ERROR level message to the log with rate limiting.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to report severe errors, such as those from which it's not possible to recover, but with rate limiting to prevent log flooding. The message will be logged at most once per default interval (see CONFIG_LOG_RATELIMIT_INTERVAL_MS).

parameterdescription.para: A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Writes an INFO level message to the log with rate limiting.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to write generic user oriented messages with rate limiting to prevent log flooding. The message will be logged at most once per default interval (see CONFIG_LOG_RATELIMIT_INTERVAL_MS).

parameterdescription.para: A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Writes a DEBUG level message to the log with rate limiting.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to write developer oriented information with rate limiting to prevent log flooding. The message will be logged at most once per default interval (see CONFIG_LOG_RATELIMIT_INTERVAL_MS).

parameterdescription.para: A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Writes an ERROR level hexdump message to the log with rate limiting.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to report severe errors, such as those from which it's not possible to recover, but with rate limiting to prevent log flooding. The message will be logged at most once per default interval (see CONFIG_LOG_RATELIMIT_INTERVAL_MS).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the data to be logged.
Length of data (in bytes).
Persistent, raw string.

briefdescription.para: Writes a WARNING level hexdump message to the log with rate limiting.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to register messages related to unusual situations that are not necessarily errors, but with rate limiting to prevent log flooding. The message will be logged at most once per default interval (see CONFIG_LOG_RATELIMIT_INTERVAL_MS).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the data to be logged.
Length of data (in bytes).
Persistent, raw string.

briefdescription.para: Writes an INFO level hexdump message to the log with rate limiting.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to write generic user oriented messages with rate limiting to prevent log flooding. The message will be logged at most once per default interval (see CONFIG_LOG_RATELIMIT_INTERVAL_MS).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the data to be logged.
Length of data (in bytes).
Persistent, raw string.

briefdescription.para: Writes a DEBUG level hexdump message to the log with rate limiting.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to write developer oriented information with rate limiting to prevent log flooding. The message will be logged at most once per default interval (see CONFIG_LOG_RATELIMIT_INTERVAL_MS).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the data to be logged.
Length of data (in bytes).
Persistent, raw string.

briefdescription.para: Rate-limited logging macros with custom rate.

detaileddescription.para: These macros provide rate-limited logging functionality with custom rate intervals. They take an explicit rate parameter (in milliseconds) that specifies the minimum interval between log messages.
Writes an ERROR level message to the log with custom rate limiting.

It's meant to report severe errors, such as those from which it's not possible to recover, but with rate limiting to prevent log flooding. The message will be logged at most once per specified interval.

parameterdescription.para: Minimum interval in milliseconds between log messages.
A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Writes a WARNING level message to the log with custom rate limiting.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant for situations that warrant investigation but could clutter the logs if output too frequently. The message will be logged at most once per specified interval.

parameterdescription.para: Minimum interval in milliseconds between log messages.
A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Writes an INFO level message to the log with custom rate limiting.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to write generic user oriented messages with rate limiting to prevent log flooding. The message will be logged at most once per specified interval.

parameterdescription.para: Minimum interval in milliseconds between log messages.
A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Writes a DEBUG level message to the log with custom rate limiting.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to write developer oriented information with rate limiting to prevent log flooding. The message will be logged at most once per specified interval.

parameterdescription.para: Minimum interval in milliseconds between log messages.
A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Writes an ERROR level hexdump message to the log with custom rate limiting.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to report severe errors, such as those from which it's not possible to recover, but with rate limiting to prevent log flooding. The message will be logged at most once per specified interval.

parameterdescription.para: Minimum interval in milliseconds between log messages.
Pointer to the data to be logged.
Length of data (in bytes).
Persistent, raw string.

briefdescription.para: Writes a WARNING level hexdump message to the log with custom rate limiting.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to register messages related to unusual situations that are not necessarily errors, but with rate limiting to prevent log flooding. The message will be logged at most once per specified interval.

parameterdescription.para: Minimum interval in milliseconds between log messages.
Pointer to the data to be logged.
Length of data (in bytes).
Persistent, raw string.

briefdescription.para: Writes an INFO level hexdump message to the log with custom rate limiting.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to write generic user oriented messages with rate limiting to prevent log flooding. The message will be logged at most once per specified interval.

parameterdescription.para: Minimum interval in milliseconds between log messages.
Pointer to the data to be logged.
Length of data (in bytes).
Persistent, raw string.

briefdescription.para: Writes a DEBUG level hexdump message to the log with custom rate limiting.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to write developer oriented information with rate limiting to prevent log flooding. The message will be logged at most once per specified interval.

parameterdescription.para: Minimum interval in milliseconds between log messages.
Pointer to the data to be logged.
Length of data (in bytes).
Persistent, raw string.

briefdescription.para: Unconditionally print raw log message.

detaileddescription.para: The result is same as if printk was used but it goes through logging infrastructure thus utilizes logging mode, e.g. deferred mode.

parameterdescription.para: A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Unconditionally print raw log message.

detaileddescription.para: Provided string is printed as is without appending any characters (e.g., color or newline).

parameterdescription.para: A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Writes an ERROR level message associated with the instance to the log.

detaileddescription.para: Message is associated with specific instance of the module which has independent filtering settings (if runtime filtering is enabled) and message prefix (<module_name>.<instance_name>). It's meant to report severe errors, such as those from which it's not possible to recover.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the log structure associated with the instance.
A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Writes a WARNING level message associated with the instance to the log.

detaileddescription.para: Message is associated with specific instance of the module which has independent filtering settings (if runtime filtering is enabled) and message prefix (<module_name>.<instance_name>). It's meant to register messages related to unusual situations that are not necessarily errors.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the log structure associated with the instance.
A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Writes an INFO level message associated with the instance to the log.

detaileddescription.para: Message is associated with specific instance of the module which has independent filtering settings (if runtime filtering is enabled) and message prefix (<module_name>.<instance_name>). It's meant to write generic user oriented messages.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the log structure associated with the instance.
A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Writes a DEBUG level message associated with the instance to the log.

detaileddescription.para: Message is associated with specific instance of the module which has independent filtering settings (if runtime filtering is enabled) and message prefix (<module_name>.<instance_name>). It's meant to write developer oriented information.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the log structure associated with the instance.
A string optionally containing printk valid conversion specifier, followed by as many values as specifiers.

briefdescription.para: Writes an ERROR level hexdump message to the log.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to report severe errors, such as those from which it's not possible to recover.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the data to be logged.
Length of data (in bytes).
Persistent, raw string.

briefdescription.para: Writes a WARNING level message to the log.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to register messages related to unusual situations that are not necessarily errors.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the data to be logged.
Length of data (in bytes).
Persistent, raw string.

briefdescription.para: Writes an INFO level message to the log.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to write generic user oriented messages.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the data to be logged.
Length of data (in bytes).
Persistent, raw string.

briefdescription.para: Writes a DEBUG level message to the log.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to write developer oriented information.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the data to be logged.
Length of data (in bytes).
Persistent, raw string.

briefdescription.para: Writes an ERROR hexdump message associated with the instance to the log.

detaileddescription.para: Message is associated with specific instance of the module which has independent filtering settings (if runtime filtering is enabled) and message prefix (<module_name>.<instance_name>). It's meant to report severe errors, such as those from which it's not possible to recover.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the log structure associated with the instance.
Pointer to the data to be logged.
Length of data (in bytes).
Persistent, raw string.

briefdescription.para: Writes a WARNING level hexdump message associated with the instance to the log.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to register messages related to unusual situations that are not necessarily errors.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the log structure associated with the instance.
Pointer to the data to be logged.
Length of data (in bytes).
Persistent, raw string.

briefdescription.para: Writes an INFO level hexdump message associated with the instance to the log.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to write generic user oriented messages.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the log structure associated with the instance.
Pointer to the data to be logged.
Length of data (in bytes).
Persistent, raw string.

briefdescription.para: Writes a DEBUG level hexdump message associated with the instance to the log.

detaileddescription.para: It's meant to write developer oriented information.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the log structure associated with the instance.
Pointer to the data to be logged.
Length of data (in bytes).
Persistent, raw string.

briefdescription.para: Create module-specific state and register the module with Logger.

detaileddescription.para: This macro normally must be used after including <zephyr/logging/log.h> to complete the initialization of the module.
Module registration can be skipped in two cases:

listitem.para: The module consists of more than one file, and another file invokes this macro. (LOG_MODULE_DECLARE() should be used instead in all of the module's other files.)
Instance logging is used and there is no need to create module entry. In that case LOG_LEVEL_SET() should be used to set log level used within the file.

detaileddescription.para:
Macro accepts one or two parameters:
module name
optional log level. If not provided then default log level is used in the file.

Example usage:
LOG_MODULE_REGISTER(foo, CONFIG_FOO_LOG_LEVEL)
LOG_MODULE_REGISTER(foo)

simplesect.para: The module's state is defined, and the module is registered, only if LOG_LEVEL for the current source file is non-zero or it is not defined and CONFIG_LOG_DEFAULT_LEVEL is non-zero. In other cases, this macro has no effect.

memberdef.name: LOG_MODULE_DECLARE
memberdef.initializer: extern const struct log_source_const_data Z_LOG_ITEM_CONST_DATA( \ GET_ARG_N(1, __VA_ARGS__)); \ extern struct log_source_dynamic_data LOG_ITEM_DYNAMIC_DATA(GET_ARG_N(1, __VA_ARGS__)); \ \ Z_LOG_MODULE_PARTITION(K_APP_DMEM) \ static const struct log_source_const_data *__log_current_const_data __unused = \ Z_DO_LOG_MODULE_REGISTER(__VA_ARGS__) \ ? &Z_LOG_ITEM_CONST_DATA(GET_ARG_N(1, __VA_ARGS__)) \ : NULL; \ \ Z_LOG_MODULE_PARTITION(K_APP_DMEM) \ static struct log_source_dynamic_data *__log_current_dynamic_data __unused = \ (Z_DO_LOG_MODULE_REGISTER(__VA_ARGS__) && \ IS_ENABLED(CONFIG_LOG_RUNTIME_FILTERING)) \ ? &LOG_ITEM_DYNAMIC_DATA(GET_ARG_N(1, __VA_ARGS__)) \ : NULL; \ \ Z_LOG_MODULE_PARTITION(K_APP_BMEM) \ static const uint32_t __log_level __unused = _LOG_LEVEL_RESOLVE(__VA_ARGS__)

briefdescription.para: Macro for declaring a log module (not registering it).

detaileddescription.para: Modules which are split up over multiple files must have exactly one file use LOG_MODULE_REGISTER() to create module-specific state and register the module with the logger core.
The other files in the module should use this macro instead to declare that same state. (Otherwise, LOG_INF() etc. will not be able to refer to module-specific state variables.)

Macro accepts one or two parameters:
module name
optional log level. If not provided then default log level is used in the file.

Example usage:
LOG_MODULE_DECLARE(foo, CONFIG_FOO_LOG_LEVEL)
LOG_MODULE_DECLARE(foo)

simplesect.para: The module's state is declared only if LOG_LEVEL for the current source file is non-zero or it is not defined and CONFIG_LOG_DEFAULT_LEVEL is non-zero. In other cases, this macro has no effect.

briefdescription.para: Macro for setting log level in the file or function where instance logging API is used.

parameterdescription.para: Level used in file or in function.

briefdescription.para: Logger API.
